name: Card Stack Deal

params.name: intensity
params.describe: deal energy — sets the fan angle and the travel distance each card covers as it lands

license: MIT (@bsmnt/scrollytelling (c) 2023 basement.studio - LICENSE file verified) + GSAP under GreenSock standard license, free for all commercial use since Webflow Apr 2025. Same rail as kinetic-text-split / parallax-depth / pinned-crossfade.
notes: Dealt-hand card reveal on the proven @bsmnt/scrollytelling rail. Cards rise and rotate into a fanned hand, staggered in ARRAY ORDER, scrubbed to scroll. The FIRST multi-child primitive (wraps:card-list): it takes an ordered array rather than one tweened node, so it declares a cardList contract (prop/min/max) that validatePrimitive enforces on the generated section - too few cards and the deal does not read, too many and the stagger window collapses. Deal timing and fan angle are computed inside from the card count; there is NO per-card timing knob, so the agent cannot desynchronise the deal. Resting fan angle is CSS on an outer wrapper while GSAP owns only the deal-in delta on an inner node, so library and stylesheet never fight over one transform. Ships a hard fallback: prefers-reduced-motion OR touch renders a plain vertical list with no fan, no overlap and no motion - a fanned row of overlapping cards on a 375px screen is unreadable, so touch gets the readable layout by design. Fewer than 2 cards degrades to the same list. Verified live by driving ScrollTrigger by hand across the scroll range: 5 cards deal in strict array order on tweens at 0/12/24/36/48% (dur 22 each), from-state opacity 0 / rotate -10deg / translateY 137.5px (yPercent 55 of a 250px card) measured exactly, all at rest by 70%, and both fallbacks confirmed to create ZERO ScrollTriggers.
